meta.title: Jonny Greenwood — Ear Clef
meta.description: Radiohead's sonic architect and one of cinema's most restless composers — from There Will Be Blood to a shelf of Oscar nominations.

hero.name: Jonny Greenwood
hero.identity: Guitar in one hand, orchestra in the other — the Radiohead multi-instrumentalist who became one of film's defining composers.

story.heading: The Story
paragraphs: Jonny Greenwood, born in 1971, is the multi-instrumentalist at the heart of Radiohead and a member of The Smile — the guitarist who is just as likely to pick up an ondes Martenot, the eerie early electronic instrument that threads through his work. But over the last two decades he has built a second career that stands entirely on its own: film composer.

It began in earnest with Paul Thomas Anderson's There Will Be Blood (2007), whose string writing — jagged, dissonant, borrowing from his concert pieces — helped redraw what a Hollywood score could sound like. The partnership with Anderson deepened across The Master, Inherent Vice, Phantom Thread, Licorice Pizza and One Battle After Another.

Beyond Anderson he scored Jane Campion's The Power of the Dog, Lynne Ramsay's You Were Never Really Here and Pablo Larraín's Spencer. The work has drawn Academy Award nominations for Best Original Score for Phantom Thread, The Power of the Dog and One Battle After Another, alongside BAFTA and Grammy nominations.

Greenwood has no artist channel of his own; his scores reach listeners through his labels, chiefly Nonesuch Records. Between films he writes for the concert hall too, in works such as Popcorn Superhet Receiver and 48 Responses to Polymorphia — a rock guitarist who kept teaching himself to be a composer, in public, one score at a time.

shows.upcomingNote: Solo and orchestral appearances are listed at songkick.com/artists/330185.
